microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned drill execution to a scalable, repeatable deployment model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a reusable playbook for continuous infrastructure validation.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating across sales, engineering, and customer stakeholders.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — demonstrating hands-on prototyping to accelerate technical execution at scale.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows by running customer-facing demos and training sessions across 14 key Azure service teams, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and establishing repeatable onboarding playbooks.

Drove product roadmap influence by conducting **11+ structured discovery interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly landing intent-based YAML generation as a prioritized initiative.

Accelerated technical knowledge retrieval by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** platform with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and improving self-service for engineering stakeholders.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ engineering teams.

Conducted technical discovery with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual effort** per region launch, then built a roadmap to automate critical paths — directly mirroring the POC scoping and stakeholder alignment work central to solutions architecture.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to surface operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact infrastructure improvements with data-backed confidence.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating cross-functional stakeholders through each deployment phase.

Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable, low-latency release model for robotics infrastructure.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process across a fast-moving, multi-team environment.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflows for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a structured CRM process that standardized customer discovery and improved conversion at each pipeline stage.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted site conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the company website and Google profile to improve top-of-funnel technical and content performance.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying margin leaks and delivering actionable recommendations to restructure cost models.

tailoring_notes: 'Repeatable deployment model' (microsoft_ft bullet 2) mirrors JD's 'repeatable deployments' and 'playbook style deployments' language directly.
'POC scoping and stakeholder alignment' (microsoft_2022 bullet 2) maps to JD's 'scoping POCs and keeping stakeholders aligned on timeline, deliverables, and next steps' responsibility.
'AI-driven code generation' + 'structured discovery interviews' (microsoft_2023 bullets 1-2) mirror JD's 'run structured discovery' and SA demo/adoption ownership requirements.
'Low-latency release model' (amazon_robotics bullet 2) connects to JD's repeated emphasis on 'low latency AI applications' and inference performance tradeoffs.
'Technical discovery' framing in microsoft_2022 bullet 2 directly echoes JD's 'technical discovery' as a core SA responsibility, grounding an older role in JD vocabulary.
